summary: High‑energy April Fool’s 1980 show with a memorable Promised Land gag; recording quality not discussed.
review: Reviewers rave about the unique instrument‑swap opening and the subsequent burst of energy that made this indoor 1980 show one of their favorites. While no specific comments on audio fidelity are given, the performance itself is described as electrifying and unforgettable.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong, enthusiastic comments about the live performance outweigh the lack of information on recording quality; most reviewers gave 4‑5 stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No reviewer mentioned sound clarity, mix, or source type.

show_quality.setlist_flow: April Fool’s gag opener with everyone on the “wrong” instrument, then a quick return to the normal setup that launched an especially high‑octane set.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Seen playing drums (and smiling) during the gag; praised as part of the show’s excitement.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland on drums, Bob Weir on keyboards for the opening gag.
band_member_comments.Drums: Multiple members (Jerry Garcia, Brent Mydland, Mickey Hart, Bill Kreutzmann) swapped to drums; highlighted as a memorable moment.
